ddls:
CREATE TABLE "process_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that collected the data */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the process */,
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the batch */,
  "h_mole_fraction" REAL /* Mole fraction of hydrogen */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Growth rate of the process */,
  "c6HH" REAL /* Concentration of c6HH */,
  "c6H_star" REAL /* Concentration of c6H* */,
  "c6_star_H" REAL /* Concentration of c6*H */,
  "c6_double_star" REAL /* Concentration of c6** */,
  "c6HM" REAL /* Concentration of c6HM */,
  "c6HM_star" REAL /* Concentration of c6HM* */,
  "c6_star_M" REAL /* Concentration of c6*M */,
  "c6B" REAL /* Concentration of c6B */,
  "quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag of the data (valid, questionable, invalid) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the data entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the sensor in the production line */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the sensor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was installed */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the sensor (active, maintenance, offline) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "process_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each process parameter */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the process parameter */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the process parameter */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units of measurement for the process parameter */,
  "threshold_min" REAL /* Minimum acceptable value for the parameter */,
  "threshold_max" REAL /* Maximum acceptable value for the parameter */,
  "tolerance" REAL /* Tolerance range for the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "chemicals" (
  "chemical_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each chemical */,
  "chemical_name" TEXT /* Name of the chemical */,
  "formula" TEXT /* Chemical formula */,
  "concentration_units" TEXT /* Units of measurement for concentration */,
  "molecular_weight" REAL /* Molecular weight of the chemical */,
  "physical_state" TEXT /* Physical state of the chemical (solid, liquid, gas) */,
  "hazardous" TEXT /* Indicates if the chemical is hazardous (yes, no) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("chemical_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data entry that triggered the alert */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the process parameter involved in the alert */,
  "alert_value" REAL /* Value of the parameter that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (critical, warning, informational) */,
  "resolved" TEXT /* Indicates if the alert has been resolved (yes, no) */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was resolved */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was triggered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES process_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES process_parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, scientist, admin) */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the system */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., login, logout, view, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "process_improvements" (
  "improvement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each improvement */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the improvement */,
  "implemented_date" TEXT /* Date when the improvement was implemented */,
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the engineer who implemented the improvement */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the improvement */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Impact of the improvement (high, medium, low) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the improvement (planned, in progress, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("improvement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_process_improvements_engineer_id FOREIGN KEY ("engineer_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that was maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance was performed */,
  "activity_description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the engineer who performed the maintenance */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance activity */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the maintenance activity */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Next scheduled maintenance date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_engineer_id FOREIGN KEY ("engineer_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictive_maintenance" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each predictive maintenance record */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor for which the prediction is made */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date when the prediction was made */,
  "recommendation" TEXT /* Recommended maintenance activity */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction */,
  "predicted_failure_date" TEXT /* Predicted date of failure */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Last update date of the prediction */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the prediction (active, resolved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictive_maintenance_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_data" (
  "historical_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical data entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the original data entry */,
  "storage_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was stored for historical analysis */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the historical data (sensor, process) */,
  "retention_period" TEXT /* Retention period for the historical data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("historical_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_historical_data_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES process_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data visualization */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the visualization */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data entry used in the visualization */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., chart, graph) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the visualization was created */,
  "visualization_title" TEXT /* Title of the visualization */,
  "visualization_description" TEXT /* Description of the visualization */,
  "shared" TEXT /* Indicates if the visualization is shared with others (yes, no) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_visualizations_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES process_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_visualizations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
